page: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 physical cores with a single thread per core and a shared CPU allocation. Local storage is bundled with 160 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and there are no dedicated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall within the average range, with read-only cached memory bandwidth reaching 1066.55 GB/sec and uncached large-block writes at 77.42 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, while medium-sized LLM inference tasks show average text generation speeds but weaker prompt processing throughput.

This server offers a balanced resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require moderate compute and memory without the premium cost of dedicated CPU threads. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs, but the fast NVMe storage and strong single-core performance make it well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, moderate database operations, and development environments.
description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation with 8 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. It includes 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and lacks a dedicated GPU. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core CPU performance, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database transactions perform at average levels. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced resource configuration. This server is a cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, moderate database tasks, and software development, though the shared CPU model presents potential performance variability compared to dedicated instances.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 160 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced general-purpose performance.
